title: Back to the Beach
wikitext:
[]: Back to the Beach             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Sep 11, 2014 2:04 pm Out of all people, why was it picked for Asuka to come out on the beach with Shinji in EoE? I get that there was some stuff to be thrown in to act as a callback for Shinji's behavior, but when you think about it, Asuka was introduced late in the game. Somehow, Anno must have considered her to grow into such an important character to Shinji (or to have with Shinji in regard to the plot) that he chose to have such an ending scene with those two characters. What do you think? Did Asuka come to take on the position of a main character with just as much relative importance as Shinji himself in order to be called back to life as one of the only two characters to return?

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Thu Sep 11, 2014 2:23 pm I asked this very same question in a thread a week or so back and it came down to five choices; 1) Asuka went to Shinji after she noticed he left Instrumentality. 2) Lilith believed that they needed each other. 3) Shinji called out for her or someone to return and she listened. 4) Anno created Asuka to be Shinji's counterpart. 5) All the above. I go with five honestly.

[]:             Quote Post by ThanatosII » Thu Sep 11, 2014 2:44 pm I think it's pretty straight forward as per Yui's words explaining that so long as you have the will to live, you can come back. Asuka is no surprise as she right up to defeat, she resented death and clearly showed major willpower to continue living. Dying means she'd become like a doll the mother hung. She still loved her mom but only loved the mother before the contact experiment. So really, Shinji didn't bring her back and neither did Rei. Asuka came back because fuck death.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Sep 11, 2014 3:14 pm ThanatosII wrote: Asuka came back because fuck death.

[]:             Quote Post by ThanatosII » Thu Sep 11, 2014 3:20 pm ^ I'm a bit confused as well of what the thread is asking but I chose to answer the easy question: "Did Asuka come to take on the position of a main character with just as much relative importance as Shinji himself in order to be called back to life as one of the only two characters to return?" So I think the OP thinks that Asuka was "called" to be brought back rather than she coming back due to my previous reasoning above.

[]:             Quote Post by Sorrow » Thu Sep 11, 2014 3:41 pm He's not asking how or why she came back "in-universe", as they say, but why Anno intended to have Asuka, of all people, come back to Shinji. He's asking "why has Asuka become the deuteragonist by the end of Evangelion when she was introduced quite late and never seemed all that important". I wasn't going to give an answer, but since I'm here explaining the question: I think Asuka was chosen because it had to be demonstrated that the future has hope - not just for humanity but for Shinji too. There had to be a signal that people can, and will, eventually return, and Asuka having a strong sense of self fits the role nicely. She is also the most likely for Shinji to have a future with, in all honesty, and so she also returns as a signal for Shinji's hope of living with others - even if all this "hope" is skewed by what occurs on the beach and how people interpret it. Since Yui, Rei and Kaworu aren't to return to him, Asuka is the closest person to Shinji--without question--and so is the best person to show us hope for his future.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Sep 11, 2014 3:41 pm OP question is asking why, out of all the other characters, Asuka was written to join Shinji. Edit: Sorrow ninja'd me.

[]:             Quote Post by ThanatosII » Thu Sep 11, 2014 4:11 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: OP question is asking why, out of all the other characters, Asuka was written to join Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Sep 11, 2014 4:25 pm Asuka's role was clearly adjusted between the original series as aired (just one of the crowd at the end), and the DC/EoE version. Coming to EoE from the OA series, there's a definite "where the fuck did that come from?" from parts of her diatribe in the hell-train sequence.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Sep 11, 2014 6:37 pm From the Void emerges the virgin born again in sin, and hers is the path, slain thrice: Once in violation, once in Sacred Battle, once in Love. In the arrogance of casting ten in his image man mocked God and made him angry. Three times three the warrior queen comes from the charnel sea, having attained heaven by violence and returned in Love to offer the ultimate sacrifice. While the boy child in the hands of the goddess wept and begged "I am not" the great queen thrice-slain said "I am" and set foot from the waters wounded from the holy tree, sacrifice of herself to herself, and when the boy child set his hands upon her she slew his wrath with wisdom: that is strength. That is power. Better to be two in hell than one in heaven.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Thu Sep 11, 2014 6:43 pm ^ Yeah but he's asking why Anno decided to have Asuka end up there with Shinji, not why they wanted to end up together.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Sep 11, 2014 6:45 pm Why they wanted to be together is the reason Anno put her on the beach. Contrast with the severed arm ending.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Thu Sep 11, 2014 7:20 pm ^ Anno wouldn't listen to their wants or needs.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Sep 11, 2014 7:49 pm ChaddyManPrime wrote: ^ Anno wouldn't listen to their wants or needs.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Sep 11, 2014 9:24 pm ChaddyManPrime wrote: Yeah but he's asking why Anno decided to have Asuka end up there with Shinji, not why they wanted to end up together.

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Thu Sep 11, 2014 9:59 pm Because Mari didn't exist yet. But seriously, who else should have come back with him? Rei wouldn't make sense. Gendou died prior to instrumentality. Rei might not be human any longer. Misato and Kaji are occupied with steaming tang sex. Yui is gone. But come to think of it, man would it have been an interesting twist if Mari was there instead of Asuka. You know, just a random girl (in the ETV & EoE universe).

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Sep 11, 2014 10:04 pm robersora wrote: But come to think of it, man would it have been an interesting twist if Mari was there instead of Asuka. You know, just a random girl (in the ETV & EoE universe).

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Fri Sep 12, 2014 12:11 am Reichu wrote: Asuka is the only one of the female principals who isn't a mother surrogate for Shinji. In a way, it makes sense for her to be there after Shinji says farewell to his mom. Of course, then she goes and caresses Shinji's face, reminding him of his mom's final caress and how he'll never see her again and etc., then he breaks down like a little baby and cries into her bosom all the same. Growing up is hard.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Fri Sep 12, 2014 12:45 am @ Reichu Reichu, I just want to say what an honor it is that you take interest in what I have to say, thank you. I also want to say I love the fanart that you do Yeah but I was talking about why Anno did it, why did he feel that Shinji needed or deserved to have someone by his side?

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Fri Sep 12, 2014 12:47 am I don't think he has anybody "by his side" as the movie ends.
